changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_change_audit_enabled to k8s_audit_networkpolicy_change_tracking for consistency with audit logging focus
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to flaw remediation and corrective actions. However, the naming of the network policy audit function needed adjustment to better reflect its focus on audit logging rather than network policy configuration itself.
naming_improvements: Use 'audit' as the resource prefix for functions primarily focused on logging and tracking changes
title: Flaw Remediation | Time to Remediate Flaws and Benchmarks for Corrective Actions
step1_reasoning: The control SI-2 (3) focuses on flaw remediation and benchmarks for corrective actions. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that automatic patch management is enforced via admission controllers, tracking flaw remediation through audit logs, monitoring network policy changes, and configuring automatic upgrades for the API server. These actions collectively ensure timely remediation of flaws and adherence to security benchmarks.
